summary: Backend API testing for PequeWorld tienda infantil. Executed 35 pytest tests covering auth (login/register/me, invalid token), categories (public list + admin CRUD + authorization), products (list/filter/search/detail/CRUD), cart (add/update/delete/auth-required), orders (full flow with stock decrement + admin status update + permissions), admin endpoints (users list, role change, dashboard stats). 35/35 PASSED (100%). All endpoints work correctly, JWT authentication enforced, role-based authorization for admin endpoints is properly validated. Stock decrements after orders. Cart clears after order creation.

minor.issue: Endpoint uses `status` as both a query param and shadows fastapi.status import inside function body - safe but slightly confusing naming. Validation works correctly.
Quantity passed as query parameter rather than body - works but unconventional; admin/customer can pass any positive int with no upper-bound check against stock.
When creating order, stock check is done per cart-item but no transaction/rollback - if a later item insertion fails, earlier stock decrements remain (acceptable for MVP).

critical_code_review_comments: auth.py: ACCESS_TOKEN_EXPIRE_MINUTES=7d is fine; consider using datetime.now(timezone.utc) instead of utcnow() (deprecated in Py 3.12).
server.py: update_order_status param name `status` shadows imported `from fastapi import status` inside function scope - works but rename to `order_status` for clarity.
server.py: create_order has no DB transaction; partial failure could leave inconsistent stock/order state. Acceptable for MVP, but document.
models.py: price is float; for COP (no decimals) prefer int to prevent rounding issues.
JWT_SECRET_KEY default fallback in auth.py should be removed - rely strictly on env var to fail fast.
Pydantic v2 deprecation: datetime.utcnow() should be replaced with datetime.now(timezone.utc).
No rate limiting on /auth/login - vulnerable to brute force.
Cart update endpoint accepts quantity > available stock without validation.
test_credentials: admin@tienda.com / admin123 (from /app/memory/test_credentials.md). Test customers are created dynamically per session with TEST_customer_<uuid>@example.com prefix.
seed_data_creation: Used existing seeded admin and seeded products/categories. Test data created during tests is prefixed with TEST_ for easy identification. Cleanup is performed where applicable; otherwise data is tagged with TEST_ prefix.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Full pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/test_pequeworld_api.py covers all 23 listed endpoints. Tests are idempotent (UUID-based emails/slugs) and can be re-run. Use REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL from /app/frontend/.env. Admin login works. All authorization checks (admin-only endpoints return 403 for customers, 401/403 for no-auth) validated. Stock decrement after order verified.
